---
title: "Introduction"
slug: v/latest/cache/introduction
---

import { Tabs, TabItem } from "@astrojs/starlight/components";

`@warlock.js/cache` is a standalone Node.js caching library. It turns slow, repeated work — database queries, API calls, heavy computations — into cheap memory reads.

```ts
// Before: every request hits the database (~50ms each)
app.get("/product/:id", async (req, res) => {
  const product = await db.products.findById(req.params.id);
  res.json(product);
});

// After: the first request hits the database, the next 999 don't (~1ms each)
app.get("/product/:id", async (req, res) => {
  const product = await cache.remember(
    `products.${req.params.id}`,
    "1h",
    () => db.products.findById(req.params.id),
  );
  res.json(product);
});
```

What's in the box:

- **8 interchangeable drivers** — Redis, Memory, Memory Extended, LRU, File, Null, Postgres (with optional pgvector), and Mock for [tests](/v/latest/cache/testing/).
- **Rich `set` options** — `ttl` / `expiresAt` / `tags` / `onConflict` / per-call `driver` / `vector` for similarity.
- **Atomic operations** — `increment`, `decrement`, `update`, `merge`, conditional writes.
- **Cache tags** — group related entries and invalidate them together.
- **List sub-API** — queues, stacks, recent-N buffers.
- **Similarity retrieval** — store embeddings alongside entries, query by meaning ([what's similarity?](/v/latest/cache/similarity/)).
- **Stampede prevention** — when 1000 concurrent requests all miss the same key, only one hits the source of truth ([what's a stampede?](/v/latest/cache/stampede-prevention/)).
- **Stale-while-revalidate** — return cached data instantly while a background refresh runs, so slow upstreams never make fast endpoints slow ([SWR guide](/v/latest/cache/swr/)).
- **Event system** — observe every cache operation.
- **Built-in metrics** — `cache.metrics()` returns hit rate, latency percentiles, per-driver breakdowns ([metrics guide](/v/latest/cache/metrics/)).

:::info Standalone Package
Works independently of the Warlock framework in any Node.js environment — Express, Fastify, NestJS, Next.js, Remix, or any other runtime that runs on Node.js.
:::

:::tip TypeScript or JavaScript
Examples use TypeScript. If you're on plain JS, just drop the `<Type>` generics — everything works identically at runtime.
:::

## Installation

<Tabs>
  <TabItem label="npm" default>

```bash
npm install @warlock.js/cache
```

  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="yarn">

```bash
yarn add @warlock.js/cache
```

  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="pnpm">

```bash
pnpm add @warlock.js/cache
```

  </TabItem>
</Tabs>

## Core Operations

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `set(key, value, ttlOrOptions?)` | Store a value with optional TTL or [rich options](/v/latest/cache/set-options/) |
| `get(key)` | Retrieve a cached value |
| `remove(key)` | Delete a cache entry |
| `has(key)` | Check if a key exists |
| `pull(key)` | Get a value and delete it |
| `forever(key, value)` | Store a value without expiration |
| `remember(key, ttl, callback)` | Get cached value or compute and cache it (with stampede prevention) |
| `flush()` | Clear all cache entries |
| `increment(key, amount)` | Atomically increment a numeric value |
| `decrement(key, amount)` | Atomically decrement a numeric value |
| `update(key, fn)` | Atomic read-modify-write for objects |
| `merge(key, partial)` | Shallow-merge a partial into a cached object |
| `list<T>(key)` | Typed list accessor (push/shift/slice/trim) |
| `many(keys)` | Retrieve multiple values at once |
| `setMany(entries, ttl?)` | Store multiple values at once |
| `removeNamespace(namespace)` | Delete all keys matching a namespace pattern |

Conditional writes — "set only if missing" or "set only if present" — are expressed via `set(k, v, { onConflict: "create" \| "update" })` and work across every driver (Redis-native, others emulated).

## Drivers

| Use Case | Recommended Driver |
|----------|-------------------|
| Production, multi-server, distributed | Redis |
| Local development, simple persistence | File |
| Fastest, ephemeral, single-process | Memory |
| Need sliding expiration | Memory Extended |
| Limited memory, want LRU eviction | LRU Memory |
| Testing, disable cache | Null |

## Advanced Features

- [Set Options](/v/latest/cache/set-options/) — rich third-arg object: `ttl`, `expiresAt`, `tags`, `onConflict`, per-call `driver`
- [Update & Merge](/v/latest/cache/update-merge/) — atomic read-modify-write for structured values
- [Cache Lists](/v/latest/cache/lists/) — queue / stack / recent-N buffer sub-API
- [Cache Tags](/v/latest/cache/tags/) — group related entries and invalidate them together
- [Atomic Operations](/v/latest/cache/atomic-operations/) — increment, decrement, and conditional writes without race conditions
- [Stampede Prevention](/v/latest/cache/stampede-prevention/) — `remember()` shares one concurrent regeneration per key
- [Events](/v/latest/cache/events/) — observe every cache operation with 9 event types
- [Errors](/v/latest/cache/errors/) — error classes and how to react

## Quick Example

```ts
import { cache, RedisCacheDriver } from "@warlock.js/cache";

cache.setCacheConfigurations({
  default: "redis",
  drivers: { redis: RedisCacheDriver },
  options: { redis: { url: process.env.REDIS_URL, globalPrefix: "shop" } },
});
await cache.init();

// Cache a product for 1 hour (human-readable duration string)
const product = await cache.remember(
  `products.${productId}`,
  "1h",
  () => db.products.findById(productId),
);

// Invalidate all cached orders for a user after checkout
await cache.removeNamespace(`orders.${userId}`);
```
